title: systests: quadlet: fixes for RHEL8
body:
Some quadlet tests are failing on RHEL8: test code was
using journalctl to check output from containers. This
fails on RHEL8, where default log driver is k8s-file.

Solution: use 'podman logs' instead. To do so, we need to
keep the containers alive (otherwise, quadlet seems to
delete them on exit). Do so by running 'top -b' (batch);
the currently-used 'top' was failing because not-a-tty.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

Tested on RHEL-8.9.0-20230712.20. @sstosh if you have the time, could you please see if this fixes the problems you're seeing?

Timeout in `systemctl start`??? 

Very stupid question, please forgive me, but do you have 100% sync between `/usr/bin/podman` and the tests you are running? Is it possible that you're running `/usr/bin/podman` from an RPM, and tests checked out from source? The `rootfs` test had some recent changes that perhaps aren't reflected in RPM-podman. Would it be possible for you to try:
```console
# make
...
# hack/bats 252
```
The `hack/bats` script takes care to use `bin/podman` and `bin/quadlet` and make other useful settings.

Thank you so much for your help in tracking this down.

Thank you for your suggestion.

I could not reproduce the `systemctl start` hang on a new RHEL-8.9 VM.

In the interests of getting this into v4.6 to address (at least some) gating-test failures, and accepting the mieux-bien tradeoff, could we get this merged please?
@Luap99 do you know what's up with the rpm builds?

```
+ go build -buildmode pie -compiler gc '-tags=rpm_crashtraceback seccomp exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper systemd libsubid  ' -ldflags '  -B 0x71b57a6a1b75208b7126436e105beadf006e40b0 -compressdwarf=false -linkmode=external -extldflags '\''-Wl,-z,relro -Wl,--as-needed  -Wl,-z,now -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-hardened-ld -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-annobin-cc1  -Wl,--build-id=sha1 -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-package-notes  '\''' -a -v -x -o bin/gvproxy ./cmd/gvproxy
WORK=/tmp/go-build1365953410
package github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/cmd/gvproxy
	imports github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pkg/virtualnetwork
	imports github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pkg/services/dhcp
	imports github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pkg/tap

	imports gvisor.dev/gvisor/pkg/sync/locking
	imports gvisor.dev/gvisor/pkg/gohacks: build constraints exclude all Go files in /builddir/build/BUILD/podman-4.5.0.rc1/gvisor-tap-vsock-407efb5dcdb0f4445935f7360535800b60447544/vendor/gvisor.dev/gvisor/pkg/gohacks
```

Gvproxy does not build with go 1.21? Nothing we can do on the podman end, we should ignore it for now.
https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/issues/232 looks relevant

I tested on the RHEL8.9NB and all quadlet tests passed.
systemctl timeout did not occurred.
